picks.narrative: A 9.6pp gap: IBP has CHW at 52.3%, the market at 43.0%. No individual baseball factor clears the bar for a headline here — this is not a single visible mismatch, it is the model pricing the side differently than the market. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): CLE's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +134 still sits inside the playable range (to +107). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

picks.narrative: IBP makes COL 56.7% fair versus a 47.8% no-vig market price — a 9.3pp gap. The largest lift is home field (~+1.1pp): Home-park and 2026 home-game context add a modest edge for COL. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +111 still sits inside the playable range (to -111). The edge is the model pricing COL differently than the market rather than one of the marquee starter/lineup/bullpen buckets — worth taking at the number, not chasing past it.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on BAL by 4.7pp — 55.4% fair against a 50.2% price. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.1pp): Kyle Bradish 3.66 xFIP vs Nick Lodolo 4.87 xFIP. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): CIN defense grades +17 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -103 still sits inside the playable range (to -106). The win condition is five-plus quality innings from Kyle Bradish and avoiding early bullpen exposure.

picks.narrative: IBP values MIN 4.7pp higher than the market — 50.1% model vs 45.4% implied. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): NYY defense grades +24 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +120 still sits inside the playable range (to +117). This is mostly a price play — the model likes the number more than the matchup, so the baseball case is mixed.

picks.narrative: The model prices KC at 48.9% to win where the no-vig market implies 45.4%, a 4.4pp disagreement. Several smaller signals stack the same way — pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.7pp), defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp) — with no single one dominating. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +125 still sits inside the playable range (to +123). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.

picks.narrative: The model sees OAK as a 56.5% side; the market is pricing 52.4%, leaving a 4.1pp edge. Several smaller signals stack the same way — lineup quality (~+1.0pp), pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.0pp), home field (~+0.7pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): MIA defense grades +29 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current -110 still sits inside the playable range (to -110). As a cluster edge rather than one dominant mismatch, it is a thinner, more fragile signal — worth sizing accordingly.
